sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! When I clicked on the gray marker at row 42, col 25, it turned BLACK! Looking at the image, I can see the leftmost horizontal bar is now black (column 2 position). So the cycle is:
- Black -> Gray -> Off (white) -> ... or
- Gray -> Black -> ...
Wait, let me trace the full cycle. Previously:
1. Black bars (B) turned to gray (w) when clicked
2. Gray bars (w) turned to... let me check what happened before
Looking at the history, I first clicked on gray markers and they disappeared (turned off/white). Then I clicked on black markers and they turned gray. Now I clicked on a gray marker and it turned black!
So the cycle seems to be: Black -> Gray -> Off -> (maybe back to Black?)
Or: Off -> Black -> Gray -> Off
Let me verify by clicking the same marker again to see if it goes off.
Actually, looking at the image, the leftmost horizontal bar (column 1) is now black, while the others are gray. This means clicking on a gray marker turned it to black.
So the cycle is: Gray -> Black -> (what happens next?)
Let me click it again to see if it goes off.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the same marker again to see the cycle
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 25}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
